title: South Dakota Voters Repeal CO2 Pipeline Law, Summit Carbon Solutions Moves Forward with Permitting Application
content: South Dakota voters on Nov. 5 rejected a law signed earlier this year that pertains to the development of carbon dioxide pipelines within the state. Summit Carbon Solutions has announced it will push forward with its proposed CO2 pipeline project and plans to apply for a permit later this month. In March, South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem signed SB 201, a bill that created new statutory requirements for regulating linear transmission facilities, including CO2 pipelines. The law also allowed counties to impose a surcharge on certain pipelines companies and established a landowner bill of rights.
author: Analysts Account
